extraction.one_line_summary: MEEUS Jordi won the Champs-Élysées sprint by approximately one inch over PHILIPSEN Jasper after three weeks of patient lead-out development.
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD team released VAN AERT before Paris when GC lead exceeded 7.5 minutes, prioritizing family over ceremonial stage.
Bora-Hansgrohe sacrificed SAGAN's sprint opportunities all Tour to build MEEUS with full lead-out, delivering only on final stage.
UAE flanked VINGEGAARD on final podium with both YATES Adam and POGAČAR, revealing tactical depth gap versus 2022.
CICCONE Giulio secured polka dots by dominating intermediate climbs until stage 20, not by final mountain finishes.

rider_signals.MEEUS Jordi: positive: won Champs-Élysées in Tour debut after receiving full lead-out support denied to SAGAN, vindicating three-week investment
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: won green jersey uncontested from stage 4, four stage wins despite losing final sprint by inch
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: 7:29 winning margin allowed releasing VAN AERT early, indicating UAE depth upgrade failed to close gap
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: rode selflessly for POGAČAR yet still secured third overall with stage 1 win, rare dual achievement
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: two stage wins and second overall, but UAE's added depth couldn't prevent repeat 7+ minute deficit
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: first Italian mountains winner in 31 years, controlled classification through intermediate points not summit finishes
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: abandoned before Paris with 7.5+ minute lead secured, team prioritized personal moment over ceremony
